provider_setup_meta.summary: Phase 1 = Brevo Starter for launch. Operational = SES primary; Resend Pro ($20/mo) backup until SES production. Resend also Phase 2 paid + lifecycle overflow.

providers.name: HubSpot
constraints: 1M contact limit (verified)
2,000 marketing emails/mo
All paid users + company email on signup
providers.notes: Phase 3 terminal — paid CRM + company email. 1M contacts.

account_setup.notes: HubSpot account configured for Phase 3 terminal CRM.

providers.name: Beehiiv
constraints: Archived — Launch lacks multi-step Email Automations (Scale $49+ required)
Unlimited sends on Launch do not replace automation builder
MCP v1 read-only — does not send lifecycle mail
providers.notes: Deprecated for lifecycle. Account/API/MCP may remain for optional newsletter; Phase 1 moved to Brevo on free tier.

account_setup.notes: API key created — Phase 1 newsletter and list management.

mcp.cursor_steps: Settings → MCP → Add server → paste connector URL → OAuth in browser

providers.name: Brevo
constraints: 300 emails/day (reserve ~50/day for CS agent)
2,000 unique contacts in active automations (free)
Reserve ~50 sends/day for CS agent (separate from operational Resend/SES lane)
providers.upgrade_trigger: Launch: upgrade to Starter before PH. Steady state: monthly sends > 16k or downgrade if signups taper.
providers.notes: Phase 1 primary — welcome, NPS, PMF, activation. Recommend Brevo Starter ($29/mo) for launch week spike; overflow to Loops/Resend if above 20k/mo.

paid_tier.name: Starter
paid_tier.notes: Recommended for Product Hunt launch — removes 300/day and 2k automation cliffs. Can downgrade later if volume tapers; high volume = more users to convert.

account_setup.notes: Upgrade to Starter ($29/mo · 20k/mo · 500k contacts) before PH — free tier risks 300/day cap on welcome + nudge + NPS same week.
account_setup.launch_action: Subscribe to Brevo Starter before Product Hunt; build Phase 1 automations on paid tier.

mcp.cursor_steps: Settings → MCP → url https://mcp.brevo.com/v1/brevo/mcp · Authorization: Bearer ${BREVO_MCP_TOKEN} (MCP token from Brevo → SMTP & API → API Keys)

providers.name: Mailgun
constraints: Deprecated — account access blocked
providers.notes: Replaced by Resend for Phase 2 paid until Mailgun access is restored.

account_setup.next_action: Use Resend for paid lifecycle; revisit Mailgun when access is restored.

providers.name: OmniSend
constraints: 250 contact cap below 500 subs year-end goal
providers.upgrade_trigger: Pool aggregate > 80% — any member may absorb overflow from any phase
providers.notes: Fallback pool member — interchangeable with other pool providers. Used when primary provider hits cap or errors.

account_setup.notes: OmniSend account created; domain verification in progress — 500 free emails/mo.
account_setup.next_action: Complete domain DNS verification in OmniSend.

providers.name: MailerLite
constraints: Contact cap binds before send cap
providers.upgrade_trigger: Pool aggregate > 80% — any member may absorb overflow from any phase
providers.notes: Fallback pool member — interchangeable with other pool providers. Used when primary provider hits cap or errors.

account_setup.notes: MailerLite account exists but login needs recovery.
account_setup.next_action: Recover MailerLite account login before using fallback pool capacity.

providers.name: EmailOctopus
constraints: Free tier: 2,500 subscribers · 10,000 emails/mo
providers.upgrade_trigger: Contacts > 2,000 — fallback pool (MailerLite, OmniSend, or Brevo) or Phase 3 transfer
providers.notes: Phase 2 (no upgrade) — 3–4 conversion emails; then Phase 3 perpetual free.

account_setup.notes: API key created — 2,500 subscribers · 10,000 emails/mo (Phase 2 conversion).

providers.name: Resend
constraints: Lifecycle free: 100/day · 3,000/mo (Phase 2 paid + fallback pool)
Operational backup (when SES sandbox): Resend Pro ~$20/mo · 50,000/mo · no daily cap
Operational + lifecycle share the same Resend account quota
providers.upgrade_trigger: Lifecycle: daily > 80 or monthly > 80% on free. Operational: subscribe Resend Pro if SES not production-ready.
providers.notes: Phase 2 paid + Phase 1 overflow on free tier. Full-list legal/incident via Resend Pro when SES unavailable.

operational_tier.name: Pro
operational_tier.notes: Recommended operational backup until AWS SES production access. Enough for full userbase blast until ~50k active users.

account_setup.notes: Upgrade to Resend Pro ($20/mo) for operational backup while SES is in sandbox; same API key.
account_setup.launch_action: Resend Pro if sending legal/outage before SES production approval.

providers.name: Loops
constraints: Early Stage free — Powered by Loops footer
Marketing / product-help fallback only — not legal or outage
providers.notes: Lifecycle fallback pool — nurture overflow when Brevo or EmailOctopus at cap.

account_setup.notes: API key created — lifecycle marketing/product-help fallback (not legal or outage).

providers.name: Amazon SES
constraints: Sandbox: 200 emails/24h until production access approved
Automated bulk blocked until production_pending=false

account_setup.next_action: Request AWS SES production access (see docs/OPERATIONAL_EMAIL_RUNBOOK.md § SES production).

provider_candidates.name: Sendy
provider_candidates.notes: Self-hosted — not SaaS free tier; evaluate separately

buckets.label: New users
buckets.definition: First day of engagement ever in the app

buckets.label: Current users
buckets.definition: Active today and at least one other day in the prior 6 days

buckets.label: Reactivated users
buckets.definition: First day back after 7–29 days away

buckets.label: Resurrected users
buckets.definition: First day back after 30+ days away

buckets.label: At-risk WAU
buckets.definition: Inactive today, active on at least one of the prior 6 days

buckets.label: At-risk MAU
buckets.definition: Inactive today and prior 6 days; active 7–29 days ago

buckets.label: Dead users
buckets.definition: No activity in the last 30 days

brevo_launch_plan.headline: Brevo Starter before Product Hunt launch
why: Launch can spike far above free tier’s 300 emails/day (welcome + activation nudge + NPS stack the same week).
500k contacts removes the 2,000 automation-entrant prune while new signups are pouring in.
One ESP for Phase 1 + CS agent — simpler than routing overflow APIs during launch week.
brevo_launch_plan.after_launch: If signups taper after PH, revisit downgrade to free. If volume stays high, keeping Starter supports conversion at scale — more users is the problem you want.
brevo_launch_plan.overflow_note: Keep Loops + Resend in the fallback pool for extreme spikes above ~20k Brevo sends/mo.

brevo_phase1.test_note: No compressed 1-minute workflow. Validate with manual template sends, real day waits, HAS_FIRST_PROMPT/HAS_TRAINING attributes, and two cohorts (stuck vs activated). See docs/BREVO_PHASE1_TEST_SETUP.md.

HAS_FIRST_PROMPT.description: True when user has at least one llm_usage row

HAS_TRAINING.description: True when user has at least one feedback_events row

automation_steps.condition: HAS_FIRST_PROMPT is false AND HAS_TRAINING is false

oasis_signup_integration.contract: After users row is created: resolve user_id + email; call integrations.brevo_phase1.enroll_user_for_phase1 (adds contact to Oasis Lifecycle list, triggers Oasis Phase 1).

brevo_phase1.deprecated_manifest_key: brevo_phase1_test renamed to brevo_phase1; use BREVO_LIFECYCLE_LIST_ID instead of BREVO_PHASE1_QA_LIST_ID

supabase_lifecycle_email.headline: Supabase Edge Functions + daily cron (Phase 1)
supabase_lifecycle_email.summary: Supabase decides who gets which email from live product data; Brevo sends templates. Replaces Brevo Oasis Phase 1 automation as the primary trigger path.
supabase_lifecycle_email.deploy_project: Oasis app Supabase (Edge Functions + cron; not Vercel)

edge_functions.name: lifecycle-send
edge_functions.role: Shared send: dedup → Brevo template API → log cs_outreach_log

edge_functions.name: lifecycle-on-signup
edge_functions.role: Database webhook on users INSERT → welcome_email

edge_functions.name: lifecycle-daily-cron
edge_functions.role: Daily cohort SQL → nudge, CS, NPS, PMF triggers

secrets.name: BREVO_API_KEY
secrets.notes: v3 xkeysib- key; whitelist Supabase egress or disable IP lock

secrets.name: BREVO_TEMPLATE_IDS
secrets.where: Supabase Edge secrets or config table
secrets.notes: JSON map trigger_name → Brevo template id

implementation_checklist.label: Map Brevo template names to numeric template IDs
Ensure Oasis signup creates users row (webhook fires)
Keep trigger spec in email_sequences.json + Email Machine UI
Optional: deactivate Oasis Phase 1 Brevo automation

testing.strategy: Invoke Edge Functions and cron manually; use Supabase test users and cs_outreach_log dedup

supabase_lifecycle_email.architecture_ascii:
users INSERT → lifecycle-on-signup → Brevo Welcome
daily cron → lifecycle-daily-cron → SQL cohorts → lifecycle-send → Brevo templates
cs_outreach_log dedup on every send

routing_rules.label: PH week: Brevo peak sends
routing_rules.notes: Scenario planner accounts for PH signup spikes on Brevo (reserve CS agent daily budget).

routing_rules.label: Fallback pool
routing_rules.trigger: Any primary provider (Brevo, EmailOctopus, Resend) > 80% cap
routing_rules.notes: Fallback pool — used when Brevo, EmailOctopus, or Resend hits cap. Route to Loops, Resend, OmniSend, or MailerLite by headroom. Migrate users to next phase OR upgrade tier when pool aggregate > 80%.

routing_rules.label: Brevo automation prune → Phase 2
routing_rules.trigger: user received 4–7 Phase 1 emails AND Brevo automation entrants > 1,600
routing_rules.notes: Move to Phase 2 paid or conversion track.

routing_rules.label: Phase 3 perpetual free
routing_rules.notes: Google sheet + Supabase email_funnel_status; 6mo inactive → delete.

routing_rules.label: Fallback triggered — ops response
routing_rules.notes: Migrate users to next funnel phase OR pay to expand the constrained primary provider tier.

routing_rules.label: Loops — marketing / product-help fallback
routing_rules.trigger: Lifecycle fallback pool active; Loops has headroom
routing_rules.notes: Exclude legal_notice and incident_notice. Powered by Loops footer on free tier.

routing_rules.label: Operational — SES primary
routing_rules.notes: Full-list blasts when production_pending=false on ses provider.

routing_rules.label: Operational — Resend backup (SES not ready)
routing_rules.trigger: ses.production_pending=true OR SES sandbox blocks bulk
routing_rules.notes: Resend Pro ($20/mo · 50k/mo · no daily cap). send_operational.py uses this path until SES production. Not free-tier 100/day.

capacity_cliffs.action: Prune users with 4–7 Phase 1 emails; route overflow to fallback pool (Loops, Resend) or Phase 2
Route to fallback member with most headroom (prefer Loops for marketing sequences); evaluate phase migration or provider upgrade
Route legal/incident to Resend Pro backup (send_operational.py); request SES production for long-term primary
Use MailerLite or Resend for marketing fallback overflow

capacity_cliffs.trigger: Paid lifecycle daily > 80 on Resend (interim Mailgun replacement)
capacity_cliffs.action: Route to fallback pool (Brevo or Loops member)
Fallback pool; cap at 3–4 conversion emails/user; accelerate Phase 3 transfer
Route Phase 1 overflow to fallback pool (Loops, Resend); defer non-urgent sends to next day

pseudo_buckets.label: All active users (operational)
pseudo_buckets.definition: users.status = active with valid email — synced for SES operational blasts (not nurture)

pseudo_buckets.label: Waitlist / acquisition
pseudo_buckets.definition: Pre-signup or launch-funnel contacts entering as new users (PH and future launches)

pseudo_buckets.label: Paid (Stripe)
pseudo_buckets.definition: Active Zen / $20/mo user_plans row from 2026-05-24 onward

pseudo_buckets.label: Limit hitters
pseudo_buckets.definition: Free users who hit daily token cap (cross-bucket monetization segment)

pseudo_buckets.label: Company email (B2B)
pseudo_buckets.definition: Email domain not in launch_config.consumer_email_domains — HubSpot sync + enterprise sequences

pseudo_buckets.label: Perpetual free
pseudo_buckets.definition: Phase 3 terminal — 7–11 lifecycle emails (4–7 Phase 1 + 3–4 Phase 2) with no upgrade; Google sheet + Supabase email_funnel_status; 6mo inactive → delete

funnel_phases.label: Phase 1 — Welcome / onboarding / activation
activation_goals: Welcome — orient new user to Oasis
Complete onboarding — import data from prior browser (Chrome, Safari, Brave, Edge, Firefox)
Train AI assistant at least once (feedback_events; earns 1,000 tokens)
Continue nurture on Brevo while automation capacity allows
funnel_phases.readiness_model: Graduated readiness — more milestones = riper for Phase 2. Training (feedback_events) signals deep unique-feature adoption beyond a first prompt. No single milestone triggers Phase 2 alone.
funnel_phases.prune_override: When Brevo nears 2,000 automation entrants: prune users who received most Phase 1 emails and sufficient days on list, even if product milestones are incomplete, to free automation slots for new signups.
exit_triggers: Graduated readiness — most milestones met (especially limit hit, training, core emails) → Phase 2 fork evaluation
Brevo automation entrants ≥1,600 — prune users with most Phase 1 emails + sufficient days, even if milestones incomplete

kpis.label: % new users who hit daily limit
Speed to first limit hit / first prompt
Median hours to first training / feedback

funnel_phases.nurture_model: Stay on Brevo while daily and automation headroom exist; track readiness milestones — do not cap nurture artificially at 4–7 if user has not reached strong readiness.
funnel_phases.exit: Phase 1 ends on upgrade, graduated readiness (most milestones), or Brevo automation prune — not a fixed email count or single milestone.
funnel_phases.notes: Recommend Brevo Starter ($29/mo) for PH launch; free tier = 300/day · 2k automation entrants. Overflow → Loops, Resend, OmniSend.

funnel_phases.label: Phase 2 — Upgraded
funnel_phases.trigger: Stripe upgrade or company email on signup
funnel_phases.notes: Resend interim (100/day · 3k/mo, replaces Mailgun) + HubSpot CRM (1M contacts).

funnel_phases.label: Phase 2 — Not upgraded
funnel_phases.exit: Phase 3 perpetual free after 3–4 emails (7–11 total with Phase 1)
funnel_phases.notes: EmailOctopus 2,500 contacts · 10k/mo. Upgrade during conversion → Phase 2 paid.

funnel_phases.label: Operational — legal & incident
funnel_phases.trigger: Manual engineer broadcast — not lifecycle automation
funnel_phases.notes: All active users via operational sync. SES primary when production-ready; Resend Pro backup until then. Not nurture.

funnel_phases.label: Phase 3 — Terminal

paths.notes: Paid user remains in HubSpot CRM
Supabase status update; 6mo inactive → account removed

funnel_phases.notes: No further lifecycle email. Paid = HubSpot. Free = sheet + Supabase flag.

fallback_pool.notes: Lifecycle overflow only — never legal/incident (use operational_pool: SES → Resend Pro). Route to Loops, Resend free tier, OmniSend, or MailerLite by headroom.

operational_pool.notes: Legal, privacy, outage only. SES primary when production-approved. Until then: Resend Pro ($20/mo · 50k/mo · no daily cap) — enough for full active userbase for years at current scale.

resend.notes: Operational backup only on Pro — not free-tier 100/day

scenario_presets_operational.label: Full userbase blast (current scale)
scenario_presets_operational.notes: At ~122 users, one Resend blast fits in daily cap. At 500+ users, plan multi-day sends or SES failover.
